use serde::Serialize;

#[derive(Serialize)]
struct UserRecord {
    name: String,
    email: String,
    #[serde(skip_serializing)]
    password_hash: String,
    #[serde(skip_serializing)]
    api_secret: String,
    #[serde(skip_serializing)]
    session_token: String,
}

#[derive(Serialize)]
struct AuditLog {
    user_id: u64,
    action: String,
    #[serde(skip_serializing)]
    auth_cookie: String,
    #[serde(skip_serializing)]
    bearer_token: String,
}

#[derive(Serialize)]
struct SafeUserView<'a> {
    name: &'a str,
    email: &'a str,
}

fn serialize_user_safe(user: &UserRecord) -> String {
    let view = SafeUserView {
        name: &user.name,
        email: &user.email,
    };
    serde_json::to_string(&view).unwrap()
}

fn log_event_safe(log: &AuditLog) {
    let json = serde_json::to_string(log).unwrap();
    std::fs::write("audit.log", json).unwrap();
}
